steps.text: These are feelings. Everyone has feelings — they change all the time. I'll describe them and you find them!
First — this face has a big smile. Everything feels good. Find it!
steps.feedback: Happy! What makes you feel happy?

steps.text: This face has tears. Something didn't go the way they wanted.
steps.feedback: Sad. Feeling sad is normal — everyone feels sad sometimes.

steps.text: This is a big hot feeling. Your face goes red and you might want to shout. What do you think it is? Find it!
steps.feedback: Angry! When you feel angry, try this — breathe in slowly... and breathe out. That helps.

steps.text: This face isn't sure if something bad is going to happen. They keep thinking about it.
steps.feedback: Worried. Talking about worries helps make them smaller.

steps.text: Now find these three — something unexpected just happened, something exciting is coming and you can't wait, and someone feels a little shy.
Find the face that shows someone is feeling unwell.
steps.feedback: Poorly. When you feel poorly, rest and cuddles help.

steps.text: Last ones — feeling a bit embarrassed, feeling really good, waving hello, and feeling pleased.
Well done. Twelve feelings — and when you know their names, you can talk about them.
